content.full_text:
F ern P olrrrcer- P RACTTcEs C orraurs s roN
428 J Street o Suire 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
September 28,2007
Blaise J. Jackson
16935 West Bemardo Drive, Suite 170
San Diego, California 92127
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-07-151
Dear Mr. Jackson:
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Dr. Glen Grayman
regarding his duties under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").1 We base our advice on the facts presented. The Commission does not act
as a finder of fact when it renders advice. (ln re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 7l; Section
831 14.) Also, please note that we base our advice solely on the provisions 
-of 
the ect.
We therefore offer no opinion on the application, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws
such as common law conflict of interest, Government code Section 1090.
QUESTTON
Does Dr. Grayman have a conflict of interest under the Act based on his
memberships on two boards of directors, one a local agency, and the other a nonprofit
corporation?
CONCLUSION
No. The Act does not prohibit holding two positions simultaneously. Moreover,
under the Act, Dr. Grayman has no economic interest, and therefore no conflict of
interest that arises from these positions.
FACTS
You are Assistant General Counsel for the Desert Health Care District ("DHCD").
DHCD is organized under Califomia Health and Safety Code section 32000, et seq. you
represent Dr. Glen Grayman as one of DHCD's elected board members.
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81fi)O through 91014. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections l8 I l0 through I 8997 of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. A-07-151
Page No. 2
Dr. Grayman is also the President of the Board of Directors for the Health
Awareness Resource Center ("HARC"). HARC is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit corporation that
provides research, analysis, and technical services to local communities to facilitate better
decision-making regarding health and quality of life. DHCD assisted in the starr-up costs
for HARC. HARC could request grant funds from DHCD in the future, a decision on
which the DHCD would vote. In your request for advice, you explained that DHCD
could derive a tangible benefit from HARC,s research.
Dr. Grayman receives no compensation for his service on the HARC Board.
Also, while Dr. Grayman is not compensated for his role as a board member for DHCD,
you stated in our telephone conversation of september lg, zoo7, that DHCD does
provide the board members with medical insurance.
You now ask whether Dr. Grayman's serving on both of these boards would
present a conflict of interest under the Act.
ANALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials ..perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests
or the financial interests of persons who have supported them." (Section g1001(b).)
Specifically, Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in
making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influenc" u -gor"*-Lntut
decision in which the official has a financial interest.
A public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental decision within the
meaning of the Act if it is reasonably foreseeable that the governmental decision will
have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on
the official, a member of his or her immediate family, or on any one of five e.-numerated
economic interests. (Section 87103; Regulation 18700(a).) The applicable economic
interests include:
1. An interest in a business entity in which a public official
has a direct or indirect investment of $2,000 or more.
(Section 871O3,(a), Regulation 1g703.1(a).) An interest in any
business entity' in which a pubric official is a director, officei
partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of
managemenr. (Section 97103(d), Regulation 1 g703. l(b).)
2. An interest in real property in which a public official has
a direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or more. (Section
87 103(b), Regulation 18703.2.)
3. Any source of income, including promised income, to the
public official thar aggregares to $500 or more within l2
monrhs prior to the decision. (Section g7r03(c), Regulation
2 Section 82005 provides: "Business entity" means any organization or enterprise operated for
profit' including but not limited to a proprietorship, partnerstrip, tirm, business trust, joint ,"rtu..,
syndicate, corporation, or association. This definition does noi include nonprofits o.-gor..r..ntal entities.


File No. A-07-151
Page No. 3
18703.3.) "Income" does not include benefit payments
received from a local agency. (Section g2030(bX2).)
4. Any source of gifts to the public official if the gifts
aggregate to $390 or more within 12 months prior to the
decision. (Section 87103(e), Regulation 19703.4.)
5. A public official also has an economic interest in his or
her personal expenses, income, assets, or liabilities, as well as
those of his or her immediate family. This is also known as
the "personal financial effects,'rule. (Section g7103,
Regularion 18703.5.)
The only economic interest that your facts possibly implicate would be Section
87103(c), source of income. The Act, however, excludes from the definition of ..income,,
any "salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem, and social security, disability or
other similar benefit payments received from a state, local, or federal government
agency. . .." (Section 82030(b)(z); see also Grigg Advice Lerter, r-02-1g4.) while
Dr. Grayman does receive health insurance benefits from his position on the DHCD
Board, this amount is excluded from the definition of "income" and does not constitute
an "economic interest."
Because the Act regulates only financial conflicts of interest, based on the facts
you provided regarding Dr. Grayman's positions with the DHCD Board and the HARC
Board, he does not cunently have a conflict of interest, as he does not have an economic
interest under the Act.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
$,,q.h J^ll^,vr*
By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division
HMRjgl

sections.question:
Does Dr. Grayman have a conflict of interest under the Act based on his
memberships on two boards of directors, one a local agency, and the other a nonprofit
corporation?
sections.conclusion:
No. The Act does not prohibit holding two positions simultaneously. Moreover,
under the Act, Dr. Grayman has no economic interest, and therefore no conflict of
interest that arises from these positions.

QUESTION: Does Dr. Grayman have a conflict of interest under the Act based on his
memberships on two boards of directors, one a local agency, and the other a nonprofit
corporation?

CONCLUSION: No. The Act does not prohibit holding two positions simultaneously. Moreover,
under the Act, Dr. Grayman has no economic interest, and therefore no conflict of
interest that arises from these positions.
